title: Metaorthemma mu-bar — case-bound configuration token (Decision 0002)
description: Episode-local token of a metaortheme type. Not the episode; not the execution event. References — never absorbs — the episode's evidence and trace. The binder is DISTINCT from the executor. SINGLE-TYPING IS THE R4 RULE: of_type is exactly one metaortheme reference (an object, never an array). Many-to-many MetaInst — one token instantiating several metaortheme types — is an UNIMPLEMENTED FUTURE EXTENSION and is rejected by this schema; a case governed by several types is recorded as several tokens.

allOf.description: role separation: either a designated executor is named or the record states why no executor is separate from the binder
a binding with no declared start is undated governance: declare effective_from or say why validity is unbounded
scope must carry the dependent claims, an explicit typed reason why no claim depends on this token, or a declared external scope model (D6: the dependent claims live outside this bundle)

of_type.description: MetaInst(mu-bar, mu) — EXACTLY ONE metaortheme type. Single-typing is the R4 rule; plural of_type is an unimplemented future extension and is rejected here.

analysis.description: Compatible(mu-bar, A(e)): binds case-specific values WITHIN the declared analysis; never overrides A without a new analysis version

binding.description: the case-specific binding map B (reference frame, tolerance value, fixture, success surface, instrument, calibration, ...) — NONEMPTY: a metaorthemma exists only where material binding exists (M1 zero-burden rule). Typed extension map: keys are domain-declared, values are scalars.

claims.description: claim ids in Q(e) that depend on this token

no_claim_dependency_reason.description: explicit reason no claim in the episode depends on this token

external_scope.description: declared external scope model for a token whose dependent claims live outside this bundle (D6 reference mode: external-registry); such a token is not bundle-locally scope-checkable and the record must say where its scope resolves

binder.description: who/what made the binding — kept distinct from the designated executor

validity_unbounded_reason.description: explicit reason this binding carries no declared start/end

designated_executor.description: executor this token designates, when applicable; the binder ROLE stays distinct even if the same actor fills both

no_separate_executor_reason.description: explicit reason no executor is designated separately from the binder

owning_episode.description: episode_id of the episode this standalone token belongs to (D3/D4): a standalone token must name its owning episode or declare scope.external_scope; embedded tokens are owned by their containing episode
